interview this morning said: 1.-THE
membership of our new union down
there is growing and it is the inten-
ton of the boys to make Lewiston
first class union camp. Mr.. Harvey
is now our president. He was county
chairman of the labor party ticket
and is in Helena now attempting to
unseat the republican senator from
Fergus county, who is opposed to la
Dor unionism."


The committee appointed by the
trades council to attend to the en-
largement of the building that is at
present occupied as the Central Labor
hall will hold a consultation tonight
with Mr.. Oppenheimer, to see if sat
isfactory arrangements can be made
Should Mr.. Oppenheimed find it i'm
possible to acCede to the request of
the unions, then steps will at once
be taken to procure new quarters I'M
til suitable location can be found
wherein to erect a labor temple. 1t
is understood, however, that the pro-
prietOrs of the present accommoda-
tions have expressed their intention
of charging them.


Within the past year the American
Federation of Labor has increased
tenfold in this city. Said Organizer
Heskett this morning: THE Ameri-
can Federation of Labor is today in
a stronger position in Spokane than
it ever was. With it are affiliated all
the international organizations be-
sides every union of Importance"
Labor union. No, 23S, of Hope, Ida
no, which was organized about two
months ago by II. L. Hughes of Spo-
kane, reports making good progress
and expects to become one of the
! strongest in the state. The union will
| give a grand picnic some time in May
on Land Pend d'Oreille, and will OK
tend an invitation to all the unions
/ In Spokane to participate in the fes-
! tiVity.
